entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Each year in elementary grades, students read biographies of influential people from church history. This course features the stories of Christians from Alveary's modern history rotation for the years (1800-1900). The selections in these grades are chosen for their accessibility for younger students to learn about the lives and ministries of great men and women of faith.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grade 1, Bible: Grade 2, Bible: Grade 3

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Those who wish to combine students in Grades 1-3 with older students should choose either this course or the Church History for grades 4-6.

rows.label: Church History: Grades 1-3
rows.books: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Muller||Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

books.title: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Muller
Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold

blocks.text:
• Connect to previous lessons by asking students to recall where they left off. 

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the previous lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Read the chapter or selection aloud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the students' pace during the lesson, rather than feeling that a particular number of pages must be completed. It is vitally important that teachers not overload students with more material than they can absorb in one sitting.

• Students who have difficulty sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Students with regular difficulty engaging often benefit from having a picture to associate with the story WHILE listening. Feel free to view the artwork during the reading rather than after the narration.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.
• Optional: Share a piece of fine art that illustrates the story.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Introduction to George Müller
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller, globe or wall map

➜ INTRO
This year in Church History, we are reading about Christians from the past who lived between the years 1800-1900 AD. You will read about people who loved God, loved the Bible, and wanted others to know the good news that salvation is found in Jesus Christ. They followed God even when it was hard, and they faced difficulty. 

The first book we will read is "The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller," where we are introduced to George Müller, a historical person who lived a life of pursuing God through faith and prayer. In the story, a fictional (made-up) character named Curly Roddy is introduced, who the authors use to introduce us to George Müller's life of faith. Curly was an orphan. What do you know about orphans? An orphan was a child whose mother and father had died, or had one parent who died, and the other parent couldn't take care of the child properly. In the late 1800s, only children who had money in their families could afford to live in an orphanage. That's why Curly lived on the streets and, in the story, tries to steal money and is hungry for food. 

Thankfully, the historical figure of George Müller loved Jesus and wanted to show that love to orphans by opening orphanages for children who may not be able to help pay for their care. George Müller was born in 1805 and became a Christian when he was 20 years old, when his friend took him to a Bible study. After that, George decided to become a missionary and moved to London, England. During that time, he met a friend, Henry Craik, who would be his friend for life as they shared the Gospel message and ministered to children. 

➜ LOCATE
⍞ Map Resource: globe or wall map
Find London, England on a globe or wall map. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
Note on p.5 (un-numbered page) "George Muller founded" - "faith is true." 
Ch.1 p.9-16 "Curly Roddy yanked" - "began to slide."

➜ VIEW
You may want to print out this picture and hang it on your timeline. 
∞ Image Link: Picture of George Müller
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1805 - George Muller is Born

• DEFINITIONS
sexton - a person whose job is to take care of a church and the area around it, and ring the church bell
pickpocket - a person who steals from people's pockets, or the actual act of stealing money

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
Escape from the Tower
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading when we met the character of Curly Roddy?

In today's reading, we learn a little more about Curly, including the fact that he had nine brothers and sisters. After their parents died, they were separated and put in different places. Sometimes orphans would be sent to workhouses, where they would have to work for long days. Others would be sent to help out in houses or businesses. Since the character of Curly did not have an adult helping him, he chose to work for a criminal and made choices that led him to running away from the man at the church. 

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Link: Church with Belfry
The image linked is of a church similar to what is being described in the book. The word "belfry" refers to where the bells were kept. Can you see the belfry in the image of the bell tower? 

➜ READ, NARRATE & DISCUSS
p.16-24  "Flipping quickly to" - "pounds are missing?"
• What does the story say happened to Curly after his parents died, and why is he now living on the streets? How do you think this led to him working for Spuds by stealing money?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Please preview the sensitive content on p.21 that talks about the father's behavior and the loss of Curly's parents.
On p.22, preview the first paragraph describing the butcher's treatment of Curly and p.23 where Curly gets punched and challenged to a fight.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
A Plan
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading? 

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
In today's reading, we'll hear about a stagecoach going from London to Bristol. The distance between the two cities is about 120 miles. A stagecoach can only go about 5-10 miles per hour, which means the trip would take between 12 and 24 hours! If we drove a car today between those two cities, it might only take us two hours. Today, we will read what plan Curly comes up with to steal some money from that stagecoach. But first, let's look at a picture of a stagecoach.

∞ Image Link: Stagecoach
• Does the stagecoach look comfortable to use for traveling? Do you think it was a secure, safe place to carry money with you for a long distance? 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.24-33  "Now Spuds frowned" - "lost his balance."

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Robbing the Stagecoach
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ INTRO
As we continue to read about Curly's story, let's also learn more about George Müller. After George graduated from college, he became a pastor of a church and married a lady named Mary. He and his friend continued to build their ministry, teaching adults and children about the Bible. 

∞ Article Link: Torchlighter George Müller Lived on Prayer
Scroll down and read the paragraph under the heading "Relying on Prayer." 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.33-41  "Just then the" - "get outta here."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Please preview the sensitive content on p.35-36, where the plan for the stagecoach robbery is laid out.

p.41-51  "The coach was" - "his own hands."

The Raid
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ INTRO
George Müller's orphanages continued to grow, and eventually he opened Ashley Downs, where five buildings were built to house the children. 
∞ Article Link: Torchlighter George Müller Lived on Prayer
Scroll down and read the paragraph under the heading "Tears of Joy and 2000 Children." 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.52-61 "By Sunday, Curly" - "yourself, do you?"
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview the policeman's comment on p.60 regarding the use of the pistol.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Mr. Walk Helps Curly
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

NOTE: A video and look at real orphan records from Ashley Downs is listed below as an optional activity if time allows. Feel free to move this to a different week or in week 11 during the "Catch Up" week's lesson time. 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.62-71 "Curly and Spuds" - "know the difference?"
• It seems like Mr. Walk wants to help Curly. Does that surprise you? Why or why not? 

OPTIONAL: 
As time allows, visit the following link to the George Müller Museum to view a short video on Müller, and to view the Downloadable Lesson Plan to learn more about the records of two orphans who came to live at Ashley Downs. 

∞ Video Link: An Introduction to George Müller
Scroll down to select the video titled "An Introduction to George Müller." 
After viewing, click on the yellow box showing "Downloadable Lesson Plan: How Life Has Changed" for a look at what two orphans who arrived at Ashley Downs brought with them as their possessions.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
Ashley Downs
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading about George Müller?

➜ INTRO
In today's reading, Curly will find himself arriving at Ashley Downs, the orphanage started by George Müller. As a reminder, when it was fully built, there were five buildings where orphan children could live. There could be up to 2,000 children living there at a time! What do you think Curly will think about this place? 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.71-80 "You mean to" - "both of you."

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Links: Girls in Front of Buildings at Ashley Downs and
Boys at Ashley Downs
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1849 Ashley Downs Opens

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Curly Tries to Settle In
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
p.81-88 "The man who" - "was otherwise bare."
• Why was Curly lonely even though he was surrounded by other boys his age?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
As an interesting historical note, the author Charles Dickens was very concerned with the condition of orphanages during this time period, as you may be familiar with in his writings. Dickens visited George Müller's orphanage and was reassured by seeing the good conditions in which the children were being cared for.

p.88-95 "Just outside their" - "get to it."

Catch Up

➜ CATCH UP
Use this time to catch up on the reading in this or another course, or explore Extra Helpings. If you have not had time to view the video and information shared in Lesson 7, you could also use this time to do that.

p.104-112 "In the morning" - "he was doing."

lessons.teacherNotes:

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
A Cheer for Curly
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
Although Curly is a fictional or pretend character in a book, there were real children who lived at Ashley Downs. Today, view the second video and look at the lesson information from the George Müller Museum to learn more about the orphan records that were kept about the children who lived at the orphanage. 

∞ Video Link: An Introduction to Orphan Records
Scroll down to view the Introduction to Orphan Records video. 
After viewing, click on the Downloadable Lesson Plan: Using Records to Understand People of the Past and read about the Pickernell Sisters. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.112-121  "After breakfast, Mr Müller" - "over, and out?"
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1898 - George Müller Dies

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Christmas for Curly
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.121-129 "Suddenly, he was" - "sing another carol."
• How did the character of Curly change from the beginning of the book to the end of the book?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
More About George Müller
⍞ Materials: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Müller

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading about George Müller?

➜ INTRO
Today, we will read the end of our book about George Müller. Remember that the character of Curly is a fictional (made-up) story of a boy who met George Müller. However, George Müller was a real, historical figure. In what ways did George Müller share the love of God with others? 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.129-132 "Curly spooned the" - "I'll stay too."
Optional: Read "More About George Müller" p.133-136.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
Adoniram the Teacher 
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about George Müller from our reading last time?

➜ INTRO
The second book we will read for Church History this year is "Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold," where we are introduced to Adoniram Judson, a historical person who lived a life of pursuing God through faith as he lived his life wanting to bring the gospel to the people in a country called Burma. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.7-14 "Having hammered in" - "think about that."
• So far in our reading, how would you describe Adoniram Judson? 
• How would you describe his faith at this point in his life?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Adoniram Judson lost many important people throughout his life, and death is discussed in his story. Notes will be given so the teacher can preview the passage and make adjustments (to skip or adapt sentences) as needed. 
In today's reading, Judson makes a new friend who then, unbeknownst to him, lies dying in the room at the inn. Note the short references to this on p.11, 12, 14. 

• IMPORTANT DATES
1788 - Adoniram Judson is born

DEFINITIONS
swot (p.14): British slang for a person who studies excessively at the expense of their social life.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Thinking About Eternity
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading about Adoniram Judson last time?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.14-22 "Papa uses words" - "this,' he instructed."
• What happened in Adoniram's life to make him change his mind about how he felt about God?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.14-20 to adapt as needed as the story of Jacob Eames continues.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
"Someone's Got to Be First"
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold, globe or wall map

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ LOCATE
⍞ Map Resource: globe, wall map, or map link
(Optional) ∞ Map Link: Map of Myanmar (Burma)
In today's reading, Adoniram learns about the country of Burma and how the people there need to hear the gospel message. Since the time of Judson, the country of Burma has had a change in its name (in 1989). It is now called Myanmar. Sometimes a map will have both names. Find the country of Myanmar (Burma) on a globe or wall map. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.23-29 "Flicking back over" - "wild romantic undertaking."
• Being the very first missionary sent from America would be a big undertaking for Adoniram! In what ways do you think he will have to prepare to get ready to go on this mission?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Pirates!
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading about Adoniram Judson?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.31-38  "On 11th January" - "she said urgently."

➜ VIEW
You may want to print out this image and put it on your wall timeline. 
∞ Image Link: Young Adoniram Judson

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
La Belle Creol
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading from last time about Adoniram and Ann Judson?

➜ INTRO
Ann and Adoniram are still making their way to Burma. While they have already faced difficulty in making their way to Burma, they will face more in today's reading. What motivates them to keep going? (Or: Why do they keep trying to reach Burma?)

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.38-47  "Then they realized" - "we should do."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.40 where Burma's tough conditions are mentioned with reference to people being killed.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
A Long Journey
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading from last time about the Judsons' journey to Burma?

➜ INTRO
The trip has been so long, and yet Adoniram and Ann still have yet to reach their destination! What would it be like to be so far from home and facing so much unknown? But the Judsons kept their eyes on their goal of reaching Burma. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.48-55 "After the two" - "with a flower!"
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview statement on p.50 where Ann is informed that Harriet and the baby have died and p.52 when Ann's baby dies. 

• IMPORTANT DATES
1812 - Adoniram and Ann arrive in Burma

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 24
lessons.body:
Exam Week 

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course. 
Questions will come from: Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Early Days
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading about Adoniram and Ann Judson from last time? 

➜ INTRO
Both Adoniram and Ann were very good at languages and learning new languages. Growing up, Ann always made friends easily, and now those gifts of being able to easily make friends and learn to speak new languages would be gifts that God would use to share the gospel message. 

➜ VIEW IMAGE
∞ Image Link: Ann Judson

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.56-63 "They walked on" - "son was buried."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.60 where Mrs Carey and her baby lose their lives when a boat overturns. And also preview the bottom of p.62-63, when baby Roger dies and Ann and Adoniram mourn.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
Sharing the Gospel
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.65-72 "I think some" - "dignified and plain."

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
∞ Image Links: Examples of Zayats 
Adoniram begins building a zayat. Why did he decide to do this? View the images of what a zayat looks like. What differences do you notice between the two examples? Which one is more similar to the one Judson probably built?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 27

The Golden Presence
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading from last time about Ann and Adoniram Judson?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.73-82 "27th June, 1819" - "questioned, sometimes forcefully."
• What are some ways Ann and Adoniram worked together as a team to minister as missionaries to the Burmese people?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
Good News and Bad News
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall from our reading about Adoniram and Ann Judson last time?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.82-90 "While his Burmese" - "India for printing."

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
War Begins
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.90-99 "There is too" - "Things looked bleak."
• As Judson describes to Dr Price the things that have been accomplished in Burma, does it seem as though all he has been through and all the work he has done is worth it or is making a difference? Why or why not?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview today's reading, where reference is made to a mother who had a mental illness (p.97 & 98) and her two daughters will come live with the Judsons. A war begins between Burma and Britain. This will lead to Adoniram being accused of being a spy (p.99).

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Adoniram In Prison
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

NOTE: Two reading selections are given as options. The first reads straight through the chapter, which the teacher should preview due to the conditions of the prison and the threat of death to the prisoners. The second reading takes shorter selections from the chapter to give the context of Adoniram in prison, but does not focus on the more difficult aspects of treatment he was facing. 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading about Ann and Adoniram Judson last time?

➜ INTRO
Adoniram is caught in the middle of a war between Burma and Britain, and because the people in government do not trust him, he is thrown into prison. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
Reading option #1: Straight through the chapter: p.101-110 "Prison Diary-Adoniram" - "by God's grace."

Reading option #2: Alternate readings for younger or more sensitive students: 
p.101 "Prison Diary-Adoniram" - "formula of arrest."
p.105 Read the "Next Day" and "Beginning October, 1824" paragraphs.
p.108-110 "In March that" - "taken to Amarapura."

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 31
lessons.body:
Danger
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson from our reading last time?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.111-116 "In March that" - "And he did."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview section on p.115-116 about the threat of the lioness and the prisoners.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
Adoniram Continues His Ministry
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

NOTE: An optional reading is given below due to the loss of Ann and two of Adoniram's children.  Please preview the material and choose the reading appropriate for your student(s). 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ INTRO
Adoniram has been facing very difficult experiences and will face more sadness in today's reading. However, he still desires in his heart to do all he can to spread the gospel of Jesus to the people he is ministering to. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.116-123 "And several days" - "world about Jesus."
Option: p.119-123, Summarize the loss that Adoniram experienced of his wife, Ann, Abby, and Maria, and begin at the second paragraph on p.119 "Adoniram established a..."
• After Ann died, Adoniram experienced heavy sadness. How did his friends and the people around him encourage him and help him through this time?
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1826 - Ann Judson dies

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Adoniram and Sarah's Missionary Work
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ INTRO
As time has gone by, Adoniram has now married Sarah, and the two of them continue to share the good news of Jesus to the people they meet. 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Link: Book of John Translated by Adoniram Judson
As we've been reading, Judson has been working on his translation of the Bible into the Burmese language. View the image of a copy of the gospel of John at the link. Do you see Judson's name on the title page? How do the letters of the Burmese language seem different than the letters you are familiar with? 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.124-132 "Is that why" - "doing us good."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1840 - Adoniram completes the translation of the Bible into the Burmese language

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
Adoniram Marries Emily
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about Adoniram Judson?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.133-141 "Although it was" - "the spoken language."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.133, as Sarah dies. Adoniram will marry his third wife, Emily, in this chapter.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 1-3 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
Adoniram's Work Is Over/Catch Up Week
⍞ Materials: Adoniram Judson: Danger in the Streets of Gold 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about our reading about Adoniram Judson from last time?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.142-146 "It was November" - "to the other." If possible, use any extra time to catch up on reading to complete the book. 
• What are some ways God was with and helped Adoniram over Adoniram's lifetime?
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1850 - Adoniram Judson dies
